description: One-pass review of crate secretx-bitwarden (/home/mark/PROJECT/crate-secretsx/secretx-bitwarden). Filed by /review-rusty-crate.
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — single-file 445-line crate with two real correctness risks (README/code mismatch, percent-decoded slash in path), one significant private-API dependency, and a substring-matching error classifier that will quietly degrade with upstream upgrades. Hot path is straightforward and well-cached.
Correctness confidence: ~70% chance of a missed bug. Least confident flags: (1) the percent-decoded-slash issue (#3) is only a real bug if Bitwarden permits '/' in project or secret names — unverified; (2) the empty-secret-value question (#10) is unverified against live Bitwarden behavior; (3) the auth_resp.authenticated == false branch (lib.rs:159-164) was not deeply audited for what other failure modes the SDK signals via that bool versus via Err. Uncertainty driven by: zero hands-on integration testing (the README and crate doc both say 'not yet integration-tested'), and the bitwarden 2.0.0 SDK's ResponseContent Display format and AccessTokenLoginResponse structure are not visible in this checkout — would require pulling bitwarden-core source to verify.
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — code is mostly idiomatic with the usual workspace consistency gaps (missing Debug, free function instead of method, error boilerplate). One real type-design improvement (replace tuple-of-Uuids with named struct). No unsafe, no macros to flag, no clippy lints in --all-features build.
Rust idiom confidence: ~85% chance of catching the real idiom issues. Least confident flags: (1) the to_string-on-Zeroizing finding (#19) — it's so marginal it's bordering on bikeshedding and was filed with the opinion label, (2) whether the duplicate resolve_*_id helpers (#21) really shouldn't be unified — judgment call. Uncertainty driven by: bitwarden 2.0.0 SDK source not pulled into ~/GIT, so I could not confirm exact request type fields and Display impls; ran cargo clippy --all-features which was clean, but did not run --feature-powerset (no features declared in this crate, so probably no-op).
title: Review: secretx-bitwarden rusty pass
